hero.question: Will Google have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
hero.prob_take: Google's collapse from 12% to 7.5% in 24 hours signals traders are pricing in OpenAI's o1 and Anthropic's Claude 4 as serious threats, treating Google's Gemini roadmap as behind the curve. At 7.5%, the market is overcorrecting on a single benchmark cycle when Google still controls the infrastructure moat and has shown ability to iterate fast, making YES a contrarian value bet if you believe "best model" stays vague enough to include multimodal or reasoning performance where Google's actually competitive.

daily_take.headline: Google's AI Crown Slips to 7.5% Odds. Smart Money Sees This Wrong.
daily_take.deck: Google dropped 4.5 points overnight as Claude 3.5 Sonnet and GPT-4o benchmarks dominate headlines. But 7.5% severely underprices the company that controls Android, Gmail, and the world's largest AI training dataset. The market is treating this like a pure model competition when Google controls the distribution. Back the favorite before the benchmark cycle swings back.
